_comment: Active research state for Auto Audio Depth Estimation - 2026-July RayDPT-validation phase. June improvement phase (champion composite ~2.03) is archived on branch 2026-June-RayDPT-improvement. This phase restarts from the ORIGINAL RayDPT baseline (f677b0f, 5ch) with THREE changes: (1) LOSS FIX - coarse-layout & low-pass loss TARGETS use MASK-WEIGHTED pooling (old avg-pool/blur diluted the target with invalid gt=0 pixels); (2) selection composite ABS_REL weight reduced (effective 0.75 -> 0.35) since abs_rel is gameable and varies most; (3) DEPTH TARGET is now PLANAR (cubemap perpendicular-Z) - the along-ray/radial rescaling was removed from prepare._depth (87b3047). compute_errors (eval metric) unchanged. Because the target changed, NO metric from before 87b3047 is comparable: this phase re-establishes every number from scratch. State files: out/results.tsv, out/hypothesis.tsv, out/hypothesis_details.tsv, studies.json; helper utils/research.py.
reset_note: Fresh from original baseline f677b0f + mask-weighted coarse/low loss targets + composite abs_rel weight 0.75->0.35 + PLANAR (cubemap) depth target.
depth_convention: planar (cubemap perpendicular-Z); stored erp_depth used as-is; radial_factor removed
metric: composite (selection) = rmse/1.6 + (1-d1)/0.46 + 0.35*abs_rel  (lower better; RMSE+d1 dominate; abs_rel de-weighted)

active_study.general_hypothesis: Before any RayDPT work the BatVision U-Net reference must be re-anchored under the PLANAR depth target, and we must know which binaural input representation the reference actually needs. Two questions: (a) how much do the derived interaural cues (ILD, cosIPD, sinIPD) add over the bare [L,R] magnitude pair; (b) does log1p magnitude compression help or hurt.
active_study.detailed_hypothesis: log1p compression equalises the dynamic range between the direct sound and the far-field echo tail, so it should help a model that must read weak late reflections to place distant surfaces -> log wins, and it should help the 2ch stack MORE than the 5ch stack (in 5ch, ILD already supplies a log-domain ratio, so part of the compression benefit is redundant). Adding ILD+IPD gives explicit interaural level/phase, which is where azimuth and hence per-direction depth structure lives -> 5ch beats 2ch at both log settings.
active_study.experiment_note: 2x2 grid on run_base.py, 1h each, sequential on one GPU: {2ch=[L,R], 5ch=[L,R,ILD,cosIPD,sinIPD]} x {use_log True, False}. E0 batvision_2ch_nolog, E1 batvision_2ch_log, E2 batvision_5ch_nolog, E3 batvision_5ch_log. Driver: utils/run_batvision_grid.sh.
active_study.decision_rule: Rank by the honest composite. The winning cell becomes the reference number RayDPT must beat. The qualitative figure shows ONE column per channel count, always the NON-LOG variant (operator instruction), regardless of which log setting wins the composite; the log variants stay in out/results.tsv. Gaps below ~0.015 composite are near-noise -> do not crown without confirmation.

backlog.idea: Re-anchor RayDPT (train.py, 5ch, flip-aug) under the planar target; this is the number the batvision grid is compared against.
Re-validate the June optimisation envelope under the fixed loss + planar target: bf16 AMP + bs32 + lr4e-4 + warmup-cosine + EMA0.995.
Re-validate the June representation wins: multi-res STFT (fine-time echo delay) + interaural coherence (direct-vs-diffuse).
Drop the vestigial pix2pix encoder tail (24.44M->7.69M, provably equivalent) - re-apply early once baseline confirmed.
